channel.title: Bloomberg Green Global
channel.description: Últimos artigos de Bloomberg Green Global

item.title: European Heat Wave Threatens to Spur Record Cooling Demand
item.description: Western Europe is baking under a heat wave that threatens to drive record cooling demand, just as warming rivers force French nuclear reactors to curb output.
item.dc:creator: Joe Wertz, Nayla Razzouk

item.title: Top EU Party Wants Carbon Market Changes to Protect Industries
item.description: The biggest political group in the European Union’s parliament is considering pushing for carbon market changes to safeguard the region’s industrial competitiveness and curb excessive price swings.
item.dc:creator: Ewa Krukowska

item.title: Australia Sees Biggest Jump in Planned Large Renewable Projects
item.description: The amount of large-scale renewables projects classified as “probable” in Australia rose the most in records going back a decade, as the country pushes forward with ambitious plans to reduce emissions and hit green targets.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Stapczynski

item.title: Data Centers Are Being Built in Areas Exposed to Extreme Weather
item.description: A significant number of data centers is being built in areas that are exposed to extreme weather risks brought on by climate change, a report by XDI has found.
item.dc:creator: Gautam Naik

item.title: India Monsoon Starts Almost 40% Short as El Niño Upends Weather
item.description: India’s monsoon has had an unusually weak start and the lackluster rainfall is likely to persist, threatening crops and industrial activity as the El Niño weather phenomenon takes hold.
item.dc:creator: Mary Hui

item.title: Jera Books Mitsui OSK Ships to Bring Low-Carbon Ammonia to Japan
item.description: Jera Co. has agreed to book two gas carriers from Mitsui OSK Lines Ltd. that will enable Japan’s largest power producer to transport low-carbon ammonia from the US and build up a supply chain for the alternative fuel.
item.dc:creator: Sing Yee Ong

item.title: Three Rugby Deaths a Reminder of Extreme Heat Risks in Malaysia
item.description: The deaths in quick succession of three rugby players in Malaysia have put renewed focus on the risks of competing in extreme heat and humidity, as climate change pushes temperatures higher across Southeast Asia.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Weeks

item.title: Tropical Storm Arthur Threatens Dangerous Flooding in US South
item.description: Tropical Storm Arthur is expected to bring “life-threatening flooding” throughout the southeastern US as it moves across Texas, leaving torrential downpours in its wake and possibly spawning tornadoes in the region.
item.dc:creator: Brian K Sullivan

item.title: Invenergy Eyes Geothermal Leases as Trump Axes Offshore Wind
item.description: Invenergy LLC is accelerating its acquisitions of US geothermal leases just as the Trump administration canceled four of the company’s offshore wind leases worth $765 million.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Ma

item.title: AI’s Growing Power Needs Are Driving Up Carbon Prices
item.description: As AI’s power demand surges, carbon prices rise and a startup launches a new geothermal plant design.
item.dc:creator: Summer Maxwell, Michelle Ma, Laura Millan
